qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Python text-format parser recursion exhaustion on nested messages
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is in Python text-format parsing (`python/google/protobuf/text_format.py`) and hinges on raw `RecursionError` during recursive descent, while the advisory and its references describe PHP runtime binary-message parsing flaws in `php/src/Google/Protobuf/Internal/CodedInputStream.php` for negative varint lengths and broken PHP recursion-limit enforcement.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is scoped to the PHP `google/protobuf` runtime and its `CodedInputStream` handling of negative varint lengths and recursion-budget enforcement during binary message parsing, whereas the session's only qualifying finding is a Python text-format parser recursion-exhaustion issue.
evidence: The session manifest lists exactly one final qualifying finding: `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_42d16e51/python_text_format_recursion_exhaustion`.

The advisory affects Packagist package `google/protobuf`; referenced fix `60e93d2d104f2af9cd345b1c6f3891d91430244a` patches `php/src/Google/Protobuf/Internal/CodedInputStream.php` to reject negative sizes passed to `readRaw()`.

Referenced fix `c8e9b27d95c6ab2d0668b5889e7dac2c477b7038` and issue `#25067` describe a PHP recursion-limit bypass in `incrementRecursionDepthAndPushLimit()`/`GPBWire::readMessage()`, not Python text-format parsing.

The qualifying finding report instead points to `python/google/protobuf/text_format.py` and public text-format APIs such as `text_format.Parse` and `proto_text.parse`, with the demonstrated effect being a raw Python `RecursionError` on deeply nested text payloads.
